title: euphrasia gracilis subsp. ericetorum
intro: Euphrasia gracilis subsp. ericetorum, also known as the heath eyebright, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Orobanchaceae, native to Scotland and Ireland. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects, which are useful in treating eye infections, wounds, and skin conditions. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, fighting off infections, and promoting wound healing, making it a valuable addition to herbal remedies. Euphrasia gracilis subsp. ericetorum can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and eye drops for topical application, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
